FEE PAID*** STIPULATION TO DISMISS CLASS CLAIMS WITHOUT PREJUDICE, VACATE HEARING ON MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION, AND STAY ACTION; AND PROPOSED ORDER (TRANSACTION ID # 78496629) FILED BY DEFENDANT PEOPLE CENTER, INC. D/B/A RIPPLING

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT PEOPLE CENTER, INC.'S, D/B/A RIPPLING, UNOPPOSED MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. THE CASE IS STAYED PENDING ARBITRATION, EXCEPT THAT PLAINTIFF MAY MAKE A NOTICED MOTION TO DISMISS CLASS CLAIMS AND AMEND TO ASSERT PAGA PENALTY CLAIMS. THE COURT OBSERVES THAT PLAINTIFF HAS FILED A DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF A REQUEST TO DISMISS THE CLASS CLAIMS. SUCH A REQUEST MUST BE MADE BY NOTICED MOTION. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Class Allegations Without Prejudice To The Putative Class; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-10-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO DISMISS CLASS ALLEGATIONS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO THE PUTATIVE CLASS

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion to dismiss class allegations without prejudice is granted for good cause shown in the moving papers. Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-05-2026 Continued To Apr-14-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Feb-23-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PEOPLE CENTER, INC.'S, D/B/A RIPPLING, MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

The unopposed motion to compel arbitration of plaintiff Cristian Hernandez's individual claims is granted. The case is stayed pending arbitration, except that plaintiff may make a noticed motion to dismiss class claims and amend to assert PAGA penalty claims. The court observes that plaintiff has filed a declaration in support of a request to dismiss the class claims. Such a request must be made by noticed motion.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
